builtins.name: concat
builtins.summary: Concatenate arrays into one.
builtins.description:
Concatenate arrays into one. Non-array args are appended as single
elements; nullish args are skipped. Useful for prepending a sentinel row.
  $concat([any_row], $map($rows, picker))   ‚Üí [any_row, ...rows]

builtins.name: includes
builtins.summary: Membership test.
builtins.description:
Membership test. `$includes(collection, item)`:
  ‚Ä¢ array      ‚Üí true when an element strictly equals `item`
  ‚Ä¢ string     ‚Üí true when `item` is a substring
  ‚Ä¢ otherwise  ‚Üí false
  $includes($amenities, 'pool')     ¬∑ $includes($title, 'villa')

builtins.name: in
builtins.summary:
Reversed-argument membership ‚Äî reads naturally as `item in collection`:
  `$in($status, ['available', 'reserved'])`  ‚Üí status is one of ‚Ä¶
Same matching rules as `$includes` (array element / string substring).
builtins.description:
Reversed-argument membership ‚Äî reads naturally as `item in collection`:
  `$in($status, ['available', 'reserved'])`  ‚Üí status is one of ‚Ä¶
Same matching rules as `$includes` (array element / string substring).

builtins.name: filter_gte
builtins.summary: Keep array items whose numeric `field` is >= `min`.
builtins.description:
Keep array items whose numeric `field` is >= `min`. Items missing the
field or with a non-number are dropped. Generic threshold filter (the
comparison `$filter` ‚Äî which only does equality ‚Äî can't express).
  $filter_gte($results, 'similarity', 0.35)
builtins.signature: (arr: unknown, field: unknown, min: unknown): unknown[]

builtins.name: compact
builtins.summary: Drop null / undefined elements from an array (keeps '', 0, false).
builtins.description:
Drop null / undefined elements from an array (keeps '', 0, false).
  $compact([a, cond ? b : null, c])  ‚Üí the present values, in order
Returns [] for non-array inputs.
examples: $compact([a, cond ? b : null, c])  ‚Üí the present values, in order

builtins.name: slice
builtins.summary: `Array.prototype.slice`.
builtins.description: `Array.prototype.slice`. Strings supported too. Defensive on non-array/string.
builtins.signature: (v: unknown, start: unknown, end?: unknown): unknown

builtins.name: take
builtins.summary: First N elements of an array (or characters of a string).
builtins.description: First N elements of an array (or characters of a string).

builtins.name: map
builtins.summary: Map each element of `arr` to a new value.
builtins.description:
Map each element of `arr` to a new value.
  ‚Ä¢ `$map(arr, 'name')`               ‚Üí arr.map(item => item.name)
  ‚Ä¢ `$map(arr, 'a.b')`                ‚Üí arr.map(item => item.a.b)        (dotted paths)
  ‚Ä¢ `$map(arr, { id: 'id',            ‚Üí arr.map(item => ({ id, label }))
                 label: 'name_ar' })`    (object pattern ‚Üí shape projection)
Returns [] for non-array inputs.

builtins.name: filter
builtins.summary: Filter elements.
builtins.description:
Filter elements.
  ‚Ä¢ `$filter(arr, 'is_active')`            ‚Üí truthy check on item.is_active
  ‚Ä¢ `$filter(arr, { kind: 'apartment' })`  ‚Üí equality match on each pair
Returns [] for non-array inputs.

builtins.name: group_by
builtins.summary: Group elements by the value of `key` (or dotted path).

builtins.name: sort_by
builtins.summary: Stable sort by field value.
builtins.description:
Stable sort by field value. `dir` is 'asc' (default) or 'desc'.
  ‚Ä¢ `$sort_by(items, 'price_egp')`         ‚Üí ascending
  ‚Ä¢ `$sort_by(items, 'created_at', 'desc')` ‚Üí newest first
Returns a new array; doesn't mutate the input.
builtins.signature: (arr: unknown, key: unknown, dir?: unknown): unknown[]

builtins.name: unique
builtins.summary: Dedupe elements.
builtins.description:
Dedupe elements. By default, primitive values are compared by equality;
with a `key`, dedupes by `item[key]` (whole objects kept on first occurrence).
  ‚Ä¢ `$unique([1, 2, 2, 3])`               ‚Üí [1, 2, 3]
  ‚Ä¢ `$unique(compounds, 'name_en')`       ‚Üí first compound per name

builtins.name: flat_map
builtins.summary: Map + flatten one level.
builtins.description:
Map + flatten one level. Same mapping semantics as `$map`.
  ‚Ä¢ `$flat_map(orders, 'items')`          ‚Üí all line items across all orders

builtins.name: count_by
builtins.summary: Count occurrences of each unique value of `key` (or dotted path).

builtins.name: format_number
builtins.summary: Number formatting via `Intl.NumberFormat`.
builtins.description:
Number formatting via `Intl.NumberFormat`. Default locale 'en-EG' matches
the existing `formatEGP` style in the codebase.
Usage in YAML: `$format_number($price)` or `$format_number($n, 'ar-EG')`.

builtins.name: format_money
builtins.summary: Currency formatter via `Intl.NumberFormat` with `style: 'currency'`.
builtins.description:
Currency formatter via `Intl.NumberFormat` with `style: 'currency'`.
Universal ‚Äî pack-agnostic; pass any ISO 4217 currency code.
  $format_money(price, 'EGP')          ‚Üí 'EGP 1,500,000'
  $format_money(price, 'USD', 'en-US') ‚Üí '$1,500,000.00'
Returns '' for non-finite numbers. Falls back to `${value} ${currency}`
when Intl rejects an unknown currency code (defensive).
builtins.signature: (value: unknown, currency: unknown, locale?: unknown): string

builtins.name: format_money_field
builtins.summary:
Currency formatter for a STORED `money` field ‚Äî the `{ value, offset }` bag an
XRM `money` field always holds (real amount = `value / offset`, `offset`
usually 100).
builtins.description:
Currency formatter for a STORED `money` field ‚Äî the `{ value, offset }` bag an
XRM `money` field always holds (real amount = `value / offset`, `offset`
usually 100). `$format_money` takes a plain number; THIS takes the stored bag,
so an author renders `record.fields.<money>` directly without dividing by hand
(the common trap ‚Äî passing the bag to `$format_number`/`$format_money` yields
a blank).
  $format_money_field($item.fields.price, 'EGP')          ‚Üí 'EGP 1,500,000'
  $format_money_field($item.fields.price, 'USD', 'en-US') ‚Üí '$1,500,000.00'
Also accepts a plain number (treated as the amount). Returns '' for anything
that is neither a finite number nor a `{ value, offset }` bag.
builtins.signature: (m: unknown, currency: unknown, locale?: unknown): string

builtins.name: format_relative_date
builtins.summary: Relative-time formatter.
builtins.description:
Relative-time formatter. Wraps `Intl.RelativeTimeFormat`.
  $format_relative_date('2026-05-18T00:00:00Z', 'ar', '2026-05-21T00:00:00Z')
    ‚Üí 'ŸÇÿ®ŸÑ 3 ÿ£ŸäÿßŸÖ'
  $format_relative_date('2026-05-18T00:00:00Z', 'en')
    ‚Üí '3 days ago'
`base` defaults to "now"; pass a fixed ISO timestamp for deterministic
tests / golden snapshots. Picks the largest unit that yields |Œî| ‚â• 1.
Returns '' for unparseable input.
builtins.signature: (iso: unknown, locale?: unknown, base?: unknown): string

builtins.name: pluralize
builtins.summary: Locale-aware plural form selection.
builtins.description:
Locale-aware plural form selection. Wraps `Intl.PluralRules`.
  $pluralize(2, { one: 'ÿ∫ÿ±ŸÅÿ©', two: 'ÿ∫ÿ±ŸÅÿ™ÿßŸÜ', many: '{count} ÿ∫ÿ±ŸÅ',
                  other: '{count} ÿ∫ÿ±ŸÅÿ©' }, 'ar')
    ‚Üí 'ÿ∫ÿ±ŸÅÿ™ÿßŸÜ'
  $pluralize(5, { one: '{count} item', other: '{count} items' })
    ‚Üí '5 items'
`forms` is an object keyed by the CLDR plural categories (`zero`,
`one`, `two`, `few`, `many`, `other`). The selected string is
interpolated for `{count}` against the input number. Returns '' for
a non-number count or non-object forms.
Pack authors who want a domain-specific label (e.g. an Arabic
"bedrooms" label) wrap this builtin in a partial that pulls the
forms object from a locale entry.
builtins.signature: (count: unknown, forms: unknown, locale?: unknown): string

builtins.name: enum_lookup
builtins.summary: Enum-table lookup.
builtins.description:
Enum-table lookup. Returns `map[value]` if present, else `fallback`,
else `value` itself. Replaces the inline `TYPE_AR[x] ?? x` pattern.
builtins.signature: (value: unknown, map: unknown, fallback?: unknown): unknown

builtins.name: cond
builtins.summary: Multi-way conditional.
builtins.description:
Multi-way conditional. Cases evaluated left-to-right; first match wins.
  `$cond([{ when: $x == 'a', then: 1 },
          { when: $x == 'b', then: 2 },
          { default: true,   then: 3 }])`
Both `when` and `then` are pre-evaluated by the expression evaluator ‚Äî
the builtin sees materialized booleans and values.

builtins.name: spread
builtins.summary:
Spread `base` then conditionally merge each `{when, value}` override
whose `when` is truthy.
builtins.description:
Spread `base` then conditionally merge each `{when, value}` override
whose `when` is truthy. Replaces the `{...base, ...(cond ? add : {})}`
pattern in TS ‚Äî and the inline-multi-line `assign:` ladders that
accumulate optional fields in YAML.
  `$spread($core, [
    { when: $is_owner,           value: { actions: [...] } },
    { when: $compound != null,   value: { compound: $compound } },
  ])`

builtins.name: shape
builtins.summary: Project + rename fields from `obj` in one call.
builtins.description:
Project + rename fields from `obj` in one call. Replaces the flat-copy
primitives (summarize_*, map_to_*) that do nothing but pick + rename.
  `$shape($compound, {
    id:      'id',
    name:    'name_ar',
    area:    'areas.name_en',           // dotted paths supported
    display: 'name_ar ?? name_en',      // NB: no expression eval ‚Äî use a real expr position
  })`
Values in the mapping are field-name STRINGS or dotted paths; not
expressions. For per-item expressions use `$map` with a similar pattern.

builtins.name: array_of
builtins.summary: Build an array with conditional elements.
builtins.description:
Build an array with conditional elements. Elements are either values
(always included) OR `{when, value}` objects (included only when when
is truthy). Replaces the `[...always, ...(cond ? [maybe] : [])]` idiom.
  `$array_of([
    { title: 'Manage', on_select: {...} },
    { when: $is_owner, value: { title: 'Edit', on_select: {...} } },
    { title: 'Cancel' },
  ])`

builtins.name: coalesce
builtins.summary: Return the first non-null/undefined arg.
builtins.description:
Return the first non-null/undefined arg. Replaces nested `??` chains:
  $coalesce($view.label_ar, $view.label_en, $view.name)
Returns undefined when every arg is nullish.

builtins.name: sum
builtins.summary:
Sum an array's finite numbers, or ‚Äî with a field-path arg ‚Äî sum that field
across an array of objects (matches `$map`/`$filter`'s field-name
convention).
builtins.description:
Sum an array's finite numbers, or ‚Äî with a field-path arg ‚Äî sum that field
across an array of objects (matches `$map`/`$filter`'s field-name
convention). Non-numbers are ignored; a non-array yields 0. The aggregation
the `+` operator can't do over a list ‚Äî e.g. ad-hoc cart-line display
totals (authoritative order/cart totals come precomputed from the
Cart/Order capability).
  $sum([1, 2, 3])              ‚Üí 6
  $sum($cart_items, 'line_total') ‚Üí 149.97

builtins.name: round
builtins.summary:
Round to `digits` decimal places (default 0), half-up ‚Äî matches JS
`Math.round`.
builtins.description:
Round to `digits` decimal places (default 0), half-up ‚Äî matches JS
`Math.round`. Returns the input unchanged when it isn't a finite number
(so a null/undefined flows through harmlessly under safe-nav).
  $round(6.25, 1)  ‚Üí 6.3
  $round(1234.56)  ‚Üí 1235

builtins.name: min
builtins.summary: Min / max of an array's finite numbers (non-numbers ignored).
builtins.description:
Min / max of an array's finite numbers (non-numbers ignored). `null` for
an empty/all-non-numeric array, so callers can guard with `$coalesce`.
  $min([3, 1, 2])  ‚Üí 1   ¬∑   $max($prices)  ‚Üí 9500000

builtins.name: get
builtins.summary: Safe nested lookup with a fallback: `$get(obj, 'a.b.c', fallback)`.
builtins.description:
Safe nested lookup with a fallback: `$get(obj, 'a.b.c', fallback)`.
Resolves a dotted path null-safely; returns `fallback` (default null)
when the path is missing/undefined. The intuitive companion to literal
member access for dynamic keys + defaults ‚Äî e.g.
  `$get($constants.mime_extensions, $m.mime_type, '.bin')`.
builtins.signature: (obj: unknown, path: unknown, fallback?: unknown): unknown

builtins.name: is_empty
builtins.summary: True for null, undefined, '', [], or {} with no own keys.
builtins.description: True for null, undefined, '', [], or {} with no own keys.

builtins.name: has_any_key
builtins.summary: True when `obj` has a defined value for any key in `keys`.
builtins.description: True when `obj` has a defined value for any key in `keys`.

builtins.name: has_all_keys
builtins.summary: True when `obj` has a defined value for every key in `keys`.
builtins.description: True when `obj` has a defined value for every key in `keys`.

builtins.name: keys
builtins.summary: Object.keys, defensively.
builtins.description: Object.keys, defensively.

builtins.name: length
builtins.summary: Length for arrays/strings, key count for objects, 0 otherwise.
builtins.description: Length for arrays/strings, key count for objects, 0 otherwise.

builtins.name: merge
builtins.summary: Shallow object merge (b's keys override a's).
builtins.description: Shallow object merge (b's keys override a's). null-safe.

builtins.name: pick
builtins.summary: Pick a subset of keys from an object.
builtins.description: Pick a subset of keys from an object.

builtins.name: omit
builtins.summary: Drop a set of keys from an object (inverse of `pick`).
builtins.description: Drop a set of keys from an object (inverse of `pick`). null-safe.

builtins.name: entries
builtins.summary: Object ‚Üí array of `{ key, value }` pairs (insertion order).
builtins.description:
Object ‚Üí array of `{ key, value }` pairs (insertion order). Inverse-ish of
building an object; pairs nicely with `$group_by` to make sections.
  $entries($group_by($items, 'area'))  ‚Üí [{ key: 'New Cairo', value: [...] }, ‚Ä¶]
Returns [] for non-object inputs.
builtins.signature: (obj: unknown): Array<{ key: string; value: unknown }>

builtins.name: keys_where
builtins.summary: Filter object KEYS by their values' truthiness.
builtins.description:
Filter object KEYS by their values' truthiness.
  ‚Ä¢ `$keys_where({ a: 1, b: 0, c: 'x' })`  ‚Üí ['a', 'c']
Returns [] for non-object inputs.

builtins.name: paginate
builtins.summary: Window an array into `{ page, total, showing_offset, has_more, next_offset }`.
builtins.description:
Window an array into `{ page, total, showing_offset, has_more, next_offset }`.
  ‚Ä¢ `$paginate(items, 0, 10)`  ‚Üí first 10 + cursor
  ‚Ä¢ `$paginate(items, 10, 10)` ‚Üí next 10 + cursor
Non-array input ‚Üí an empty page (total 0, no more). `offset`/`limit`
coerce to safe non-negative numbers; a non-positive `limit` yields an
empty page while preserving `total` (callers can still show "0 shown of N").
builtins.signature: (items: unknown, offset: unknown, limit: unknown): PageEnvelope

builtins.name: matches
builtins.summary: Regex test.
builtins.description:
Regex test. `$matches(text, pattern, flags?)` ‚Üí boolean.
  $matches($msg, '[0-9]{7,}')            ‚Üí true when 7+ consecutive digits
  $matches($msg, 'foo', 'i')             ‚Üí case-insensitive
Non-string text / pattern or an invalid pattern ‚Üí false.
builtins.signature: (text: unknown, pattern: unknown, flags?: unknown): boolean

builtins.name: count
builtins.summary: Count regex matches.
builtins.description:
Count regex matches. `$count(text, pattern, flags?)` ‚Üí number. Always
counts globally (the `g` flag is added if absent).
  $count($msg, '[0-9Ÿ†-Ÿ©]')     ‚Üí how many real digits in the text
examples: $count($msg, '[0-9Ÿ†-Ÿ©]')     ‚Üí how many real digits in the text
builtins.signature: (text: unknown, pattern: unknown, flags?: unknown): number

builtins.name: normalize_digits
builtins.summary:
Fold non-ASCII decimal digits to ASCII 0-9 (Arabic-Indic Ÿ†-Ÿ© + Persian
ğ∞-ğπ).
builtins.description:
Fold non-ASCII decimal digits to ASCII 0-9 (Arabic-Indic Ÿ†-Ÿ© + Persian
ğ∞-ğπ). `$normalize_digits('Ÿ†Ÿ°Ÿ¢')` ‚Üí '012'. Other characters pass through.

builtins.name: translate
builtins.summary: 1:1 character translation (SQL-`translate` style).
builtins.description:
1:1 character translation (SQL-`translate` style). Each char in `from` is
replaced by the char at the same index in `to`; chars absent from `from`
pass through. Used for leet-speak folding, diacritic stripping, etc.
  $translate($msg, 'OolI', '0011')       ‚Üí fold O/o‚Üí0, l/I‚Üí1
`to` shorter than `from`: the unmatched `from` chars map to '' (deleted).
builtins.signature: (text: unknown, from: unknown, to: unknown): unknown

builtins.name: truncate
builtins.summary:
Truncate a string to at most `max` characters, appending `ellipsis`
(default '‚Ä¶') when truncation happens.
builtins.description:
Truncate a string to at most `max` characters, appending `ellipsis`
(default '‚Ä¶') when truncation happens. Returns the string unchanged
when shorter than `max`.
  $truncate('long title here', 8)       ‚Üí 'long ti‚Ä¶'
  $truncate('short', 8)                 ‚Üí 'short'
  $truncate('long', 8, '...')           ‚Üí 'long'    (no trunc needed)
  $truncate('very long title', 8, '...') ‚Üí 'very ...'
Returns '' for non-string input. The ellipsis counts against `max`, so
the output is never longer than `max`.
builtins.signature: (str: unknown, max: unknown, ellipsis?: unknown): string

builtins.name: summarize_items
builtins.summary:
Summarize an array as a single-line breadcrumb ‚Äî universal helper for
memory_note fields.
builtins.description:
Summarize an array as a single-line breadcrumb ‚Äî universal helper for
memory_note fields. Each element renders the `template` with `{path}`
placeholders resolved against the element via dotted path lookup.
  $summarize_items($views, '{reference} {city} [{status}]',
    { max: 5, separator: ' | ' })
    ‚Üí 'UID-A city1 [available] | UID-B city2 [sold] | ‚Ä¶'
Defaults: `max = 5`, `separator = ' | '`. Returns '' for non-array
inputs. Path lookups are null-safe; missing paths render as ''.
NOTE: the template uses bare `{path}` (NOT `{$item.path}`) because
expressions are already evaluated by the time the builtin runs. The
per-item substitution happens inside this function against each item.
builtins.signature: (arr: unknown, template: unknown, opts?: unknown): string

builtins.name: unit_ref
builtins.summary: UUID ‚Üí `UID-XXXXXXXX` reference string.
builtins.description:
UUID ‚Üí `UID-XXXXXXXX` reference string. Mirrors `utils/ref.ts:unitRef`.
Pure string slicing ‚Äî safe to use inline in YAML expressions.

builtins.name: lead_ref
builtins.summary: UUID ‚Üí `LID-XXXXXXXX` lead reference.
builtins.description: UUID ‚Üí `LID-XXXXXXXX` lead reference. Mirrors `utils/ref.ts:leadRef`.

builtins.name: lines
builtins.summary: Join parts into a single string, filtering only null and undefined.
builtins.description:
Join parts into a single string, filtering only null and undefined.
Empty strings are PRESERVED ‚Äî they act as intentional layout separators
(e.g. blank lines between sections of a card body). Default separator
is '\n'. Replaces the `const lines: string[] = []; if (x) lines.push(...);
return lines.join('\n')` pattern from primitives ‚Äî pure transforms
become inline YAML. Phase 6 Win A; semantics relaxed in Phase 8 so
formatter migrations preserve empty-line layout.
Usage in YAML:
  `'$lines([$header, $body, $footer])'`              ‚Üí '\n' separated
  `'$lines($parts, " | ")'`                          ‚Üí custom separator
  `'$lines([$a, ($b > 0 ? $b_label : null)])'`       ‚Üí conditional segments (null is dropped)
  `'$lines([$header, "", $body])'`                   ‚Üí blank line between header and body
To drop empty strings too (legacy Phase-6 behavior), filter the array
before passing: `$lines($parts.filter(p => p))`.

builtins.name: url_encode
builtins.summary: URL-encode a string (encodeURIComponent) ‚Äî e.g.
builtins.description: URL-encode a string (encodeURIComponent) ‚Äî e.g. building wa.me share links.

builtins.name: trim
builtins.summary: Trim leading/trailing whitespace.
builtins.description: Trim leading/trailing whitespace. Returns '' for non-string input.

builtins.name: lower
builtins.summary: Lowercase a string (locale-agnostic).
builtins.description: Lowercase a string (locale-agnostic). Returns '' for non-string input.

builtins.name: upper
builtins.summary: Uppercase a string (locale-agnostic).
builtins.description: Uppercase a string (locale-agnostic). Returns '' for non-string input.

builtins.name: join_with
builtins.summary:
Join an array into a human-readable list with separator + optional
"and" connector for the last element:
  $join_with(['a', 'b', 'c'], ', ', ', and ')   ‚Üí 'a, b, and c'
  $join_with(['only one'], ', ', ', and ')      ‚Üí 'only one'
  $join_with(['a', 'b'], ', ', ' and ')         ‚Üí 'a and b'
When `last_sep` is omitted, behaves like `Array.prototype.join`.
builtins.description:
Join an array into a human-readable list with separator + optional
"and" connector for the last element:
  $join_with(['a', 'b', 'c'], ', ', ', and ')   ‚Üí 'a, b, and c'
  $join_with(['only one'], ', ', ', and ')      ‚Üí 'only one'
  $join_with(['a', 'b'], ', ', ' and ')         ‚Üí 'a and b'
When `last_sep` is omitted, behaves like `Array.prototype.join`.
Null / undefined elements are dropped; remaining values stringified.
builtins.signature: (arr: unknown, sep: unknown, last_sep?: unknown): string

builtins.name: first_n
builtins.summary: First N WORDS of a string (vs `$truncate`'s char-based cut).
builtins.description:
First N WORDS of a string (vs `$truncate`'s char-based cut). Splits
on whitespace runs; preserves single-space joining. Appends `ellipsis`
(default '‚Ä¶') when truncated.
  $first_n('the quick brown fox jumps', 3)        ‚Üí 'the quick brown‚Ä¶'
  $first_n('short', 5)                            ‚Üí 'short'
  $first_n('', 3)                                 ‚Üí ''
examples: $first_n('the quick brown fox jumps', 3)        ‚Üí 'the quick brown‚Ä¶'
builtins.signature: (str: unknown, n: unknown, ellipsis?: unknown): string

builtins.name: slug
builtins.summary: Make a URL-safe slug from a string.
builtins.description:
Make a URL-safe slug from a string. Lowercases ASCII, collapses
runs of non-alphanumeric chars to `-`, trims leading/trailing `-`.
Preserves Unicode letters/digits (so `ÿ≥ŸÑÿßŸÖ ÿπŸÑŸäŸÉŸÖ` ‚Üí `ÿ≥ŸÑÿßŸÖ-ÿπŸÑŸäŸÉŸÖ`).
  $slug('Hello World')           ‚Üí 'hello-world'
  $slug('  Foo --- Bar! ')       ‚Üí 'foo-bar'
  $slug('ÿ≥ŸÑÿßŸÖ ÿπŸÑŸäŸÉŸÖ')             ‚Üí 'ÿ≥ŸÑÿßŸÖ-ÿπŸÑŸäŸÉŸÖ'
  $slug('')                      ‚Üí ''

builtins.name: t
builtins.summary: Pack-locale string lookup for the run's language. Keys MUST be namespaced (`<pack_id>.‚Ä¶` / `<flow_id>.‚Ä¶`) ‚Äî a bare key returns the raw string unchanged (lint-enforced).
builtins.description: Pack-locale string lookup for the run's language. Keys MUST be namespaced (`<pack_id>.‚Ä¶` / `<flow_id>.‚Ä¶`) ‚Äî a bare key returns the raw string unchanged (lint-enforced). (Runtime-bound: provided per run by FlowTool (locale/pack context), not an evaluator builtin.)

builtins.name: t_has
builtins.summary: True when the pack locale defines the (namespaced) key for the run's language ‚Äî guard optional copy before `$t`.
builtins.description: True when the pack locale defines the (namespaced) key for the run's language ‚Äî guard optional copy before `$t`. (Runtime-bound: provided per run by FlowTool (locale/pack context), not an evaluator builtin.)

builtins.name: enum_label

builtins.name: record_url
